D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Blue-Light Filter on the SPD

a warm filter does its circadian work in a narrow 460–480 nm window — exactly where the non-visual photoreceptor peaks

A display emits light in three peaks — a red, a green, and a blue primary. The circadian system responds through melanopsin in ipRGCs, which peaks near 480 nm, overlapping the blue primary. A blue-light filter attenuates that short-wavelength region, lowering the melanopic-weighted output more than the visible brightness.

Raise the filter strength and watch the blue peak shrink while the melanopsin curve stays fixed. The readout tracks how much melanopic stimulus is removed — far more than the loss of overall light, because the filter targets the band the clock cares about.
